ddls:
CREATE TABLE "memory_regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each memory region */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the memory region */,
  "address_range" TEXT /* Address range of the memory region */,
  "size" INTEGER /* Size of the memory region */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Access permissions for the memory region */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the memory region was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the memory region */,
  "active" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the memory region is active */,
  "region_type" TEXT /* Type of memory region (e.g., stack, heap, code) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "mpu_registers" (
  "register_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each MPU register */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the MPU register */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the MPU register */,
  "limit" INTEGER /* LIMIT field value */,
  "c" INTEGER /* C field value */,
  "perm" INTEGER /* PERM field value */,
  "rd" INTEGER /* RD field value */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the MPU register was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the MPU register */,
  "active" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the MPU register is active */,
  "register_type" TEXT /* Type of MPU register (e.g., control, status) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("register_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_mpu_registers_limit FOREIGN KEY ("limit") REFERENCES permissions ("permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "register_fields" (
  "field_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each field */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the field */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the field */,
  "register_id" INTEGER /* ID of the MPU register the field belongs to */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the register field was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the register field */,
  "default_value" INTEGER /* Default value for the field */,
  "editable" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the field can be edited */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("field_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission level */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the permission level */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the permission level */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the permission level was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the permission level */,
  "default_permission" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if this is a default permission level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_control" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access control setting */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* ID of the memory region */,
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* ID of the permission level */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the access control setting */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the access control setting was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the access control setting */,
  "active" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the access control setting is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_control_permission_id FOREIGN KEY ("permission_id") REFERENCES permissions ("permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_control_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES memory_regions ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, developer, admin) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the user was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the user */,
  "active" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the user is active */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user's role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* ID of the memory region */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the memory region */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the access event */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., read, write, execute) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the access log was created */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the memory region */,
  "successful" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the access attempt was successful */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES memory_regions ("region_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "security_threats" (
  "threat_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each security threat */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the security threat */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity of the security threat (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the security threat detection */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the security threat was detected */,
  "resolved_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the security threat was resolved */,
  "resolved_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who resolved the security threat */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the security threat (e.g., open, in progress, resolved) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("threat_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_security_threats_resolved_by FOREIGN KEY ("resolved_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "memory_usage" (
  "usage_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each memory usage statistic */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* ID of the memory region */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the memory usage statistic */,
  "usage" REAL /* Memory usage percentage */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the memory usage statistic was recorded */,
  "peak_usage" REAL /* Peak memory usage percentage during the recorded interval */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("usage_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_memory_usage_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES memory_regions ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "threat_id" INTEGER /* ID of the security threat (if applicable) */,
  "usage_id" INTEGER /* ID of the memory usage statistic (if applicable) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the notification */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the notification */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the notification was created */,
  "sent_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the notification was sent */,
  "read_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the notification was read */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the notification (e.g., unread, read, archived) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_threat_id FOREIGN KEY ("threat_id") REFERENCES security_threats ("threat_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_usage_id FOREIGN KEY ("usage_id") REFERENCES memory_usage ("usage_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the role was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the role */,
  "default_role" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if this is a default role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)
